authors.name: Portugal Active Team
authors.bio: The team behind Portugal Active — sharing local knowledge, property stories, and travel inspiration.

authors.name: Ricardo Viana
authors.bio: Founder and CEO of Portugal Active. Born in the Minho, former national downhill MTB champion, passionate about sharing Portugal's hidden corners.

articles.title: Viana do Castelo: One of the Most Welcoming Cities in the World
articles.content:
Booking.com analysed over 309 million reviews to determine the most welcoming cities on Earth — and Viana do Castelo ranked third in the world. For those of us who call this place home, the recognition came as no surprise.

Viana do Castelo is a city where history and nature converge in ways that few places can match. The Basilica de Santa Luzia crowns the hilltop with panoramic views that stretch across the Lima River estuary to the open Atlantic. Below, the city centre is a living museum of Manueline architecture, Art Nouveau facades, and cobblestone streets that lead to one of the most beautiful waterfronts in Portugal.

But what earned Viana its ranking isn't the architecture — it's the people. This is a city where the fishmonger at the market remembers your name, where the café owner brings you a pastel de nata before you order it, and where strangers offer directions before you've finished unfolding the map. The warmth here is cultural, not performative.

The natural setting amplifies everything. Viana sits where the mountains meet the river meets the ocean, creating a playground for outdoor pursuits that ranges from surfing and stand-up paddleboarding to mountain biking and hiking. The beaches north of the city — Cabedelo, Afife, Moledo — are among the most beautiful in the country, with golden sand and consistent Atlantic waves.

Every August, the Romaria de Nossa Senhora d'Agonia transforms the city into one vast celebration. It's the biggest traditional festival in Portugal, with processions, traditional costumes, fireworks over the river, and a spirit of communal joy that captures everything Viana represents.

Portugal Active properties in the region consistently achieve review scores between 9.4 and 10. When your guests love the destination as much as we do, the hospitality comes naturally.
articles.excerpt: Booking.com named it the third most welcoming city in the world. Here's why Viana do Castelo deserves the recognition.
articles.seoTitle: Viana do Castelo — One of the Most Welcoming Cities in the World
articles.seoDescription: Ranked third most welcoming city by Booking.com: discover Viana do Castelo's natural beauty, cultural warmth, and outdoor adventures.

articles.title: Video: Showcasing Viana do Castelo in Collaboration with CMVC

author.name: Ricardo Viana
author.bio: Founder and CEO of Portugal Active.

articles.content:
In partnership with the City Hall of Viana do Castelo (CMVC), Portugal Active produced this film showcasing the natural wonders and cultural richness of the region. From the coastal trails and river experiences to the cuisine and cultural heritage, this video captures the essence of what makes northern Portugal so extraordinary.

The collaboration brought together local knowledge with international production values, highlighting the experiences that Portugal Active offers: cultural tours through the historic centre, water sports on the Lima River and Atlantic coast, cycling routes through the countryside, and the accommodations that serve as a base for exploring it all.

Connect With Life Again.
articles.excerpt: A partnership between Portugal Active and the City Hall of Viana do Castelo, showcasing the region's natural beauty and cultural heritage.
articles.seoTitle: Video: Portugal Active Showcasing Viana do Castelo
articles.seoDescription: Watch Portugal Active's collaboration with Viana do Castelo City Hall — a cinematic showcase of the Minho region's nature, culture, and adventure.

articles.title: Getaway: The Best Things to Do in Viana do Castelo
articles.content:
Featured in Time Out magazine, this guide covers the essential experiences for anyone visiting Viana do Castelo and the surrounding Minho coast.

For dining, the region punches well above its weight. O Manel is a local institution for traditional Minhota cuisine, while Tasquinha da Linda — whose owners are also fish wholesalers — has earned a listing in the Michelin Guide. And no visit is complete without the pastries at Confeitaria Manuel Natário, a café that has been perfecting its craft for generations.

Adventure is woven into the landscape here. Horseback riding along the coast, sailing trips on the Atlantic, and Can-Am routes through the Serra da Nó mountain range offer something for every level of thrill-seeker. The variety of terrain — ocean, river, forest, mountain — means you can pack a remarkable range of experiences into a single day.

For accommodation, Portugal Active's Connected Lodge offers a premium base for exploring the region. With four bedrooms accommodating up to twelve guests, the property features a fully equipped kitchen, expansive garden with swimming pool, and the kind of thoughtful details — from the fireplace to the badminton court — that make a house feel like home.
articles.excerpt: Featured in Time Out: dining, adventure, and the best experiences in Viana do Castelo and the Minho coast.
articles.seoTitle: Best Things to Do in Viana do Castelo — A Getaway Guide
articles.seoDescription: Michelin Guide restaurants, horseback riding, sailing, and mountain adventures: your complete guide to Viana do Castelo.

articles.title: Forbes: Portugal's Adventure Travel Paradise Away From the Crowds
articles.content:
Forbes magazine featured Portugal Active and the Minho region in an in-depth piece exploring the rise of immersive adventure travel in lesser-known destinations. The article highlighted our philosophy of combining premium accommodation with authentic, locally-rooted experiences — a model that addresses the growing demand for travel that goes beyond the conventional.

The feature focused on how the Minho region offers a genuine alternative to Portugal's more crowded destinations. While the Algarve and Lisbon capture most of the international attention, the Minho delivers an experience that's richer, more personal, and more connected to Portuguese culture.

For Portugal Active, the Forbes recognition was a milestone. It validated the approach we've taken since day one: that the best travel experiences come from knowing a place deeply, and from offering guests access to the hidden corners and local knowledge that no guidebook can provide.

We're grateful for the recognition and more committed than ever to our mission of putting the Minho on the world's adventure travel map.
articles.excerpt: Forbes magazine features Portugal Active and the Minho region as an adventure travel paradise away from the crowds.
articles.seoTitle: Forbes Features Portugal Active — Adventure Travel in the Minho
articles.seoDescription: Forbes magazine highlights Portugal Active's Minho experiences as an adventure travel paradise away from the crowds.

articles.title: Neopop: Viana do Castelo's Electronic Music Festival
articles.content:
Neopop Electronic Music Festival is one of the most important electronic music events in Portugal, drawing international techno artists to perform in a setting that's utterly unique: the historic Santiago da Barra castle overlooking the Atlantic Ocean in Viana do Castelo.

The combination of world-class music with the atmospheric fortress and the ocean as a backdrop creates an experience that regular festival-goers rank among Europe's best. The intimate scale and the quality of the lineup attract a knowledgeable crowd that's there for the music.

Beyond the festival, Viana do Castelo offers the perfect complement: the city's maritime heritage, its Michelin-recognised dining scene, and outdoor adventures from bike tours along the waterfront to sailing on the Atlantic. Tasquinha da Linda, whose owners are also fish wholesalers, is a local favourite for a post-festival meal.

Portugal Active homes make an ideal base for Neopop. Instead of a festival campsite, return to a private villa with a pool, a stocked kitchen, and the comfort of a five-star experience. It's the best way to combine world-class electronic music with the kind of rest and recovery the festival demands.
articles.excerpt: One of Portugal's most important electronic music festivals takes place in a 16th-century castle overlooking the Atlantic.
articles.seoTitle: Neopop Festival Viana do Castelo — Where to Stay & What to Expect
articles.seoDescription: Neopop Electronic Music Festival in Viana do Castelo's Santiago da Barra castle: lineup, atmosphere, and where to stay.

articles.title: Video: Forget Lisbon and Porto, THIS Is the REAL Portugal!
articles.content:
When YouTube creators Joen and Amelie — with over 100,000 subscribers — visited Portugal Active, they didn't head for Lisbon or Porto. Instead, they came to the Minho to discover what they called the real Portugal.

Staying at the Connected Lodge, Joen and Amelie explored the Alto Minho region with Portugal Active as their guides. From the wild beaches to the mountain trails, from local cuisine to water sports, they captured the authentic side of Portugal that most international visitors never see.

Their video is a testament to what happens when you step off the tourist trail and into a region where the hospitality is genuine, the landscapes are untouched, and every day brings a new adventure. As they put it, the experience created countless moments of pure joy and memories for a lifetime.
articles.excerpt: YouTube creators Joen and Amelie discover the real Portugal in the Minho with Portugal Active.
articles.seoTitle: Video: Forget Lisbon and Porto — This Is the Real Portugal
articles.seoDescription: YouTube creators explore the Minho with Portugal Active and discover the authentic side of Portugal beyond Lisbon and Porto.

articles.title: The Guardian: Where to Stay and What to Do Off the Beaten Track
articles.content:
The Guardian featured Armada's House — one of Portugal Active's signature properties — in an article exploring hidden Portugal. The piece, titled "Hidden Portugal: where to stay and what to do off the beaten track," highlighted the villa's historical connection to the Vasco da Gama family and its transformation into a premium holiday home.

The Guardian's recognition of Armada's House as a standout destination for travellers seeking authenticity over tourism is a testament to the Portugal Active approach: properties with genuine character, in locations that reward the curious traveller.

The article is available in The Guardian's travel section for those who want to explore the full feature.
articles.excerpt: The Guardian features Armada's House in an article exploring Portugal's hidden gems off the beaten track.
articles.seoTitle: The Guardian Features Portugal Active — Hidden Portugal
articles.seoDescription: The Guardian highlights Armada's House by Portugal Active in a feature on hidden Portugal destinations off the beaten track.

articles.title: Video: Portugal Active Stand Up Paddle Experience
articles.content:
When our guests are asked to describe the SUP Sun Experience in a single word, the answer is always the same: magic.

The Stand Up Paddle experience takes participants along the Lima River, beginning at the medieval town of Ponte de Lima — one of the oldest towns in Portugal — and concluding at the beaches of Cabedelo where the river meets the Atlantic. Along the way, paddlers witness either a remarkable sunrise or sunset from the centre of the river, surrounded by the green hillsides and historic bridges that define this valley.

SUP can be adapted to paddlers of all levels, from complete beginners to experienced riders. Our guides know the river intimately and adjust the pace, route, and stops based on conditions and the group's experience. It's one of the most peaceful and visually stunning experiences we offer.
articles.excerpt: Paddle the Lima River from Ponte de Lima to the Atlantic — our guests call it magic.
articles.seoTitle: Video: Stand Up Paddle on the Lima River — Portugal Active
articles.seoDescription: SUP from Ponte de Lima to the Atlantic coast: watch the Portugal Active Stand Up Paddle experience on the Lima River.

articles.title: Video: Portugal Active Private Yoga Session
articles.content:
Work on your strength, flexibility, and balance with our private Yoga and Pilates sessions. Whether you're a complete beginner, a seasoned practitioner, or anywhere in between, our classes are customised to your level and preferences.

Held in the gardens of our properties or on the beach at sunrise, these sessions are the ultimate way to start or end your day. Our certified instructors bring the equipment and create a practice tailored to you — no crowded studio, no fixed schedule, just movement and breath in one of the most beautiful settings in Portugal.

It's one of the most requested experiences by our guests, and once you've done yoga overlooking the Atlantic, it's hard to go back to the gym.
articles.excerpt: Private yoga and pilates in the garden or on the beach — customised sessions for every level.
articles.seoTitle: Video: Private Yoga Session — Portugal Active
articles.seoDescription: Private yoga and pilates sessions at Portugal Active: personalised classes in stunning garden and beachside settings.

articles.title: Men's Health: Paddle, Yoga and Bike Tours in Viana do Castelo
articles.content:
Just one year after Portugal Active's founding in 2017, Men's Health magazine highlighted the company's nature and sports-based experiences in Viana do Castelo. The publication featured the range of outdoor activities available through Portugal Active, from stand-up paddle tours along Cabedelo beach to bike routes through the coastal areas and natural trails.

The article noted that Portugal Active's experiences, starting from just €35, include a professional guide, all necessary equipment, and refreshments — making adventure accessible to anyone visiting the region.

The early recognition from a major health and fitness publication validated the vision behind Portugal Active: that the Minho's natural landscape is one of Europe's best-kept secrets for active, outdoor-focused holidays.
articles.excerpt: Men's Health magazine features Portugal Active's nature and sports experiences in Viana do Castelo, just one year after launch.
articles.seoTitle: Men's Health Features Portugal Active — Paddle, Yoga & Bike Tours
articles.seoDescription: Men's Health covers Portugal Active's outdoor experiences in Viana do Castelo: SUP, yoga, and bike tours from €35.

articles.title: Video: Portugal Active Sailing Experience
articles.content:
Enjoy a sailing trip with your best company along the Lima River mouth and the seaside coast of Viana do Castelo. Present your family with a different and unique experience, marked by strong emotions and views that only the water can provide.

On a sailboat, nothing is ordinary. The perspective shifts entirely when you're on the water — the coastline reveals hidden coves, the Santa Luzia basilica glows in the afternoon light, and the rhythm of the waves sets a pace that's impossible to find on land.

Our sailing experiences are suitable for all ages and experience levels. Whether you want to learn the basics of handling a sailboat or simply relax on deck while our skipper navigates the coast, the experience adapts to you.
articles.excerpt: Sail the Lima River mouth and the Viana do Castelo coastline — on a sailboat, nothing is ordinary.
articles.seoTitle: Video: Sailing Experience — Portugal Active
articles.seoDescription: Sail the Lima River and Viana do Castelo coast with Portugal Active. A unique family experience on the Atlantic.

articles.title: Video: Portugal Active — De Norte a Sagres

author.name: Ricardo Viana
author.bio: Founder and CEO of Portugal Active.

articles.content:
From the northernmost coast to the southernmost point of mainland Portugal — this is Portugal Active's journey from Norte to Sagres, produced in collaboration with Fábrica PT.

The film captures the full diversity of Portugal's landscape and the spirit of adventure that drives everything we do. From the green mountains of the Minho to the wild Atlantic coast, from the Douro Valley vineyards to the golden cliffs of the Algarve, this is Portugal as we know it.

Embrace extraordinary. Where luxury meets the horizon. Designed for escape.
articles.excerpt: From the Minho to Sagres — Portugal Active's cinematic journey across the entire Portuguese coast.
articles.seoTitle: Video: De Norte a Sagres — Portugal Active Adventure
articles.seoDescription: Watch Portugal Active's cinematic journey from northern Portugal to Sagres, capturing the full diversity of the Portuguese coast.

articles.title: Alto Minho Ativar IPVC Business Awards 2020

author.name: Ricardo Viana
author.bio: Founder and CEO of Portugal Active.

articles.content:
Portugal Active received the Marketing Innovation Award at the Gala Alto Minho Ativar IPVC Business Awards 2020. This annual event, organised by CEVAL and the Polytechnic Institute of Viana do Castelo (IPVC), has recognised outstanding practices in innovation, management, and entrepreneurship within the Alto Minho region for over a decade.

Receiving this distinction was a proud moment for the entire team. It recognised the work we've put into building a brand that represents not just accommodation, but a complete reimagination of how people experience the Minho coast.

Thank you to CEVAL and IPVC for the recognition, and to all those who were and are part of this journey.
articles.excerpt: Portugal Active receives the Marketing Innovation Award at the Alto Minho Ativar IPVC Business Awards 2020.
articles.seoTitle: Portugal Active Wins Marketing Innovation Award — IPVC Business Awards
articles.seoDescription: Portugal Active receives the Marketing Innovation Award at the Alto Minho Ativar IPVC Business Awards 2020.

articles.title: Booking.com Traveller Review Awards 2020

author.name: Ricardo Viana
author.bio: Founder and CEO of Portugal Active.

articles.content:
Portugal Active received the 2020 Traveller Review Awards from Booking.com with a score of 9.5 out of 10. For a company that started as a personal passion project, this recognition carried special meaning.

Anyone who knows me knows that everything is done with a very strong purpose. Portugal Active began as a dream — to share the Minho coast with the world in a way that does justice to its beauty and its people. A 9.5 score from our guests tells us we're on the right track.

The Booking.com Traveller Review Awards are based entirely on guest feedback, which makes them particularly meaningful. No jury, no application process — just the honest opinions of the people who stayed with us.

Thank you to every guest, every team member, and every supporter who has been part of this journey. The dream continues to grow.
articles.excerpt: Portugal Active scores 9.5/10 in the Booking.com Traveller Review Awards 2020 — recognition built entirely on guest feedback.
articles.seoTitle: Booking.com Traveller Review Awards 2020 — Portugal Active 9.5/10
articles.seoDescription: Portugal Active scores 9.5 out of 10 in the 2020 Booking.com Traveller Review Awards.

articles.title: Portugal Active Featured in Grand Tour Portugal
articles.content:
Portugal Active was featured in Grand Tour Portugal, a premium publication that showcases the best of Portuguese hospitality, culture, and design. The feature highlighted our distinctive residential properties around Viana do Castelo — homes with clean, contemporary design positioned in privileged coastal and mountain locations.

The article presented Portugal Active as a new approach to hospitality in the region, offering visitors an alternative to traditional hotel establishments with accommodations that combine architectural elegance, privacy, and direct access to the natural surroundings that make the Minho coast extraordinary.
articles.excerpt: Grand Tour Portugal features Portugal Active's premium properties in Viana do Castelo — contemporary design in privileged natural settings.
articles.seoTitle: Portugal Active Featured in Grand Tour Portugal
articles.seoDescription: Grand Tour Portugal showcases Portugal Active's contemporary design properties in Viana do Castelo's coastal and mountain settings.

articles.title: Youtuber for a Day: Fat Bike Wild Experience EP.1
articles.content:
Thanks to our sponsorship by GoPro, we challenged our guests to become YouTubers for a day. The Cantaert family were the first to take up the challenge, documenting their Fat Bike Wild Experience through the Minho landscape.

Fat bikes — mountain bikes with oversized tyres designed for sand and rough terrain — are one of Portugal Active's most popular adventures. The routes take riders through forest trails, along wild beaches, and across terrain that regular bikes can't handle. It's mountain biking without limits.

The resulting video captures the joy of discovery: a family exploring a new landscape together, pushing their comfort zones, and returning with GoPro footage and memories that perfectly represent the Portugal Active spirit.
articles.excerpt: The Cantaert family take on the Fat Bike Wild Experience with GoPro cameras — Portugal Active's first Youtuber for a Day challenge.
articles.seoTitle: Video: Fat Bike Wild Experience EP.1 — Youtuber for a Day
articles.seoDescription: Watch the Cantaert family's Fat Bike Wild Experience in the Minho — GoPro-filmed adventure through forests and beaches.

articles.title: NIT: Portugal Active Ocean Lodge
articles.content:
NiT (New in Town), one of Portugal's leading lifestyle and tourism publications, featured the Portugal Active Ocean Lodge in an in-depth piece by journalist Patrícia Naves. The article highlighted the property's location in Vila Praia de Âncora, set within more than eight thousand square metres of land and just 1,200 metres from the beach.

What captured the journalist's attention was the property's private river access, which allows guests to reach nearby beaches via kayak, stand-up paddleboard, or surfboard within 15 minutes. The article emphasised the experiential offerings that come with every stay: on-demand services including a private chef, massage therapy, fitness training, and yoga instruction.

The NiT feature brought Portugal Active Ocean Lodge to a national audience and validated our approach to hospitality: properties that are destinations in themselves, not just places to sleep.
articles.excerpt: NiT features the Ocean Lodge: 8,000m² of private land, river access to the beach, and on-demand luxury services.
articles.seoTitle: NiT Features Portugal Active Ocean Lodge — Vila Praia de Âncora
articles.seoDescription: NiT magazine profiles Portugal Active Ocean Lodge: 8,000m² property in Vila Praia de Âncora with private river access and luxury services.

articles.title: TimeOut: Portugal Active Ocean Lodge
articles.content:
TimeOut Magazine featured the Portugal Active Ocean Lodge in an article about the Minho region. The piece, part of a broader feature on the region, gave extensive praise to the property — describing its stone architecture, expansive 9,000-square-metre garden, and the atmosphere that makes guests reluctant to leave.

The journalists noted that the property felt like a paradise, and that the combination of the natural setting, the quality of the accommodation, and the warmth of the hospitality created an experience that was genuinely hard to leave behind.

The TimeOut feature reached a broad national readership and helped establish the Ocean Lodge as one of the Minho's standout accommodation experiences.
articles.excerpt: TimeOut Magazine profiles the Ocean Lodge — a stone residence in a 9,000m² garden that journalists called paradise.
articles.seoTitle: TimeOut Features Portugal Active Ocean Lodge — Minho
articles.seoDescription: TimeOut Magazine's feature on Portugal Active Ocean Lodge: stone architecture, 9,000m² garden, and a paradise in the Minho.

articles.title: Evasões: A Dream Mansion to Rent in Viana do Castelo
articles.content:
Evasões, the premium travel supplement distributed with the Portuguese newspaper Jornal de Notícias, featured Portugal Active with an article celebrating the guest experience. The piece highlighted the combination of premium services — including private chef — alongside the adventure activities that define the Portugal Active offering.

The journalist was particularly struck by the panoramic views over the mountains and the valley, and described the Portugal Active Lodge architecture as a dream mansion. For a publication known for its high editorial standards in travel and lifestyle, the feature represented significant recognition.

The Evasões article reached a national audience of travel enthusiasts and established Portugal Active's properties as a reference point for premium holiday accommodation in northern Portugal.
articles.excerpt: Evasões magazine calls the Portugal Active Lodge a dream mansion — premium services and panoramic views in Viana do Castelo.
articles.seoTitle: Evasões: Dream Mansion in Viana do Castelo — Portugal Active
articles.seoDescription: Evasões magazine features Portugal Active's dream mansion: panoramic mountain views, private chef, and adventure in Viana do Castelo.

articles.title: Guest Diary: Family Time in Paradise
articles.content:
A multi-generational family holiday at Portugal Active Lodge, told in the guests' own words. From the ultimate seafood feast to endless lunch platters, from searching for Cristiano Ronaldo football shirts to queuing for local pastries, this diary captures the joy of a family discovering the Minho together.

The adventure highlight was the Tour de Viana: seven fat bikes, a cable car ride, and mountain terrain guided by BMX champion Ronaldo Ricardo. The family swam in a secret cove, devoured ice cream and Portuguese custard tarts, and successfully built a fire that became the evening's centrepiece.

As the sun set over the Lima River and the cameras came out for the obligatory family photos, the mood was one of complete contentment. This is what family holidays are supposed to feel like — unhurried, adventurous, and full of the kind of moments that become stories told at future dinner tables.
articles.excerpt: A family's diary from their stay at Portugal Active Lodge: fat bikes, seafood feasts, secret coves, and sunsets over the Lima River.
articles.seoTitle: Guest Diary: Family Time in Paradise — Portugal Active Lodge
articles.seoDescription: A family shares their Portugal Active experience: fat bike tours, seafood feasts, secret swimming coves, and Lima River sunsets.

articles.title: Our New Hidden Paradise: Portugal Active Ocean Lodge

author.name: Ricardo Viana
author.bio: Founder and CEO of Portugal Active.

articles.content:
The reveal of Portugal Active's Ocean Lodge — a property that redefined what we could offer our guests. Set in Vila Praia de Âncora with private river access leading to the beach, this video takes you inside the property for the first time.

The Ocean Lodge is more than accommodation. It's an experience base: a private estate where the river flows through the grounds, where kayaks and paddleboards launch from your garden, and where the Atlantic is just a 15-minute paddle downstream.

This is our hidden paradise. And now it's yours to discover.
articles.excerpt: The reveal of Portugal Active Ocean Lodge — private river access, beachfront adventure, and a new standard in Minho hospitality.
articles.seoTitle: Video: Portugal Active Ocean Lodge — Our Hidden Paradise
articles.seoDescription: Discover Portugal Active Ocean Lodge: private river access, paddle to the beach, and luxury in Vila Praia de Âncora.

articles.title: Guest Diary: Our First Time in Portugal
articles.content:
Adrian Lomas, founder and CEO of Blueleaf, and Alexandra Tyrer, project manager at McLaren Applied Technologies, chose Viana do Castelo for their first ever trip to Portugal. What they found exceeded every expectation.

The adventure started with fat tire bikes on the beach — deflated tyres for the sand dunes, a technique that felt completely alien to these London-based professionals. Portugal Active founder Ricardo Viana, a former national downhill MTB champion, personally guided them through the experience. Stand-up paddleboarding down the Lima River towards Viana became a highlight they still talk about.

The days filled up naturally: temple exploration by bike, swimming in natural pools, horseback riding along the beach, and dinners at local restaurants where the food was as honest as the welcome. Adrian summed it up simply: this guy really cares and loves the area.

For Adrian and Alexandra, Portugal wasn't what they expected. It was better.
articles.excerpt: Adrian and Alexandra's first trip to Portugal: fat bikes, SUP on the Lima River, and a founder who genuinely cares about the region.
articles.seoTitle: Guest Diary: First Time in Portugal — Portugal Active Experience
articles.seoDescription: Adrian and Alexandra share their first Portugal experience: fat bikes, SUP on the Lima River, horseback riding, and Minho hospitality.

articles.title: Guest Diary: From Barcelona to Portugal Active
articles.content:
Three sports enthusiasts from Barcelona — Frederico, Núria, and Enric — travelled to Viana do Castelo in June 2018 for kitesurfing and mountain biking. What they found was an experience that went far beyond sport.

From the first moment, Ricardo helped with everything. The accommodation surpassed expectations: a property with a fireplace ready, refreshments waiting, and every detail considered. Dinner at Taberna Afonso delivered what Frederico called the best cod roast he'd ever tasted.

The mountain biking took them along the Lima River eco-route to Ponte de Lima — a fantastic ride beside the river between fields and trees, followed by a challenging 600-metre climb with 20% gradients to the summit. The afternoon brought kitesurfing at Cabedelo beach in perfect conditions.

The verdict was unanimous: mind-blowing scenery, maximum comfort, great support, nature, sea, beach, adventure, friends, sports, and lots of fun. Everything a sports-focused holiday should be.
articles.excerpt: Three Barcelona sports enthusiasts discover the Minho: mountain biking, kitesurfing, and the best cod roast they've ever tasted.
articles.seoTitle: Guest Diary: From Barcelona to Portugal Active — Kitesurf & MTB
articles.seoDescription: From Barcelona to Viana do Castelo: mountain biking the Lima eco-route, kitesurfing at Cabedelo, and premium Minho hospitality.

articles.title: Men's Health: Portugal Active Takes Kitesurf to Higher Education
articles.content:
Men's Health magazine covered Portugal Active's initiative to introduce kitesurfing into the higher education curriculum in partnership with the Polytechnic Institute of Viana do Castelo (IPVC). This was a world first: kitesurfing had never been part of a higher education programme anywhere.

The project's mission was the culturalisation and solidification of extreme sports in Viana do Castelo and in Portugal. By bringing kitesurfing into an academic setting, Portugal Active aimed to establish the region as a recognised centre for water sports, combining practical training with the study of sports tourism, environmental science, and outdoor education.

The partnership demonstrated Portugal Active's commitment to the Minho region that goes beyond tourism — investing in the educational infrastructure that will develop the next generation of outdoor sports professionals.
articles.excerpt: A world first: Portugal Active partners with IPVC to introduce kitesurfing into higher education.
articles.seoTitle: Men's Health: Portugal Active Takes Kitesurf to Higher Education
articles.seoDescription: Men's Health covers Portugal Active's world-first partnership with IPVC to bring kitesurfing into the higher education curriculum.
